original: Original "Star Wars" movie (Episode IV – A New Hope), directed byGeorge Lucasand starringMark Hamill,Carrie FisherandHarrison Ford, premieres
First ever albino panda footage in the wild released (taken in April) from China's Wolong National Nature Reserve
JFKannounces the US goal of putting a man on the Moon before the end of the decade
ASpaceXDragon becomes the first commercial spacecraft to dock at the International Space Station
32-year-old Erik Weihenmayer, of Boulder, Colorado, becomes the first blind person to reach the summit of Mount Everest
"Return of the Jedi" (Star WarsEpisode VI), produced byGeorge Lucasfirst released
Goofy, aka Dippy Dawg, 1st appears in 'Mickey's Revue' by Walt Disney
"Wonder Woman" directed by Patty Jenkins, starringGal Gadotand Chris Pine premieres in Los Angeles - 1st superhero film directed by a woman
Legendary American athleteJesse Owensequals or breaks four world records in 45 minutes at a Big Ten meet at Ferry Field in Ann Arbor, Michigan; remembered as "the greatest 45 minutes ever in sport"
Kirk Gibson (Tigers) & Jorge Orta (Blue Jays) hit inside park HRs

Frank Oz [Oznowicz], American muppetteer (Sesame Street - "Grover"; " Cookie Monster"; "Bert"; The Muppet Show - "Miss Piggy", Fozzie Bear"), film director (Little Shop of Horrors, What About Bob?; Bowfinger), and actor, born in Hereford, England
Dorothy Straight, American 4-year-old author (How the World Began), born in Washington, D.C.
95-year-old woman scores a hole-in-one in Florida
Casey Stengelreleases a sparrow from under his baseball cap
1st US National Missing Children's Day is proclaimed
Weird Al Yankovicrecords parody song "She Drives Like Crazy"
Stan Sakai, Japanese-American cartoonist (Usagi Yojimbo), born in Kyoto, Japan
Bob Gale, American screenwriter (Back to the Future), born in University City, Missouri
Christian Dopplerpresents his idea, now known as the Doppler Effect (through the changing colors of binary stars), to the Royal Bohemian Society, Prague
US launches 1st Skylab crew Kerwin, Conrad, Weitz
Arthur C. Clark proposes relay satellites in geosynchronous orbit
Ivan Bella, Slovak cosmonaut who was the 1st Slovak citizen to fly in space, born in Brezno, Czechoslovakia
Henry Fordannounces that he is ending production of the Model T Ford
Muhammad AliKOs Sonny Liston at 2:12 of round 1 at Central Maine Civic Center, Lewiston to retain his WBC/WBA heavyweight boxing title
Formula 1 driverLando Norriswins the Monaco Grand Prix for McLaren, the team's first victory at the event since 2008
NASA civilian pilotJoseph A. Walkertakes X-15 to 32,770 m
Ruthie Bolton, American basketball guard (Olympic gold 1996), born in Lucedale, Mississippi
Irena Szewińska, Polish 200m runner (Olympic Gold 1964, 68, 76), born in Leningrad, Russian SSR, Soviet Union
K. C. Jones, American Basketball Hall of Fame point guard (Olympic gold 1956; NBA C'ship 1959–66, Boston Celtics) and coach (NBA C'ship 1984, 86; Boston Celtics), born in Taylor, Texas (d. 2020)
Todd Stottlemyre andMel Stottlemyrebecome first father and son to win 100 games in Major League Baseball
Boston Red SoxCarl Yastrzemski(41) is 4th to play 3,000 MLB baseball games (Cobb, Musial & Aaron)
Martín Dihigo, Cuban Baseball HOF two-way player (2 × Negro League All-Star New York Cubans; 4 × Cuban League MVP), born in Matanzas, Cuba (d. 1971)
Series of 19 twisters destroy Udall, Kansas and most of Blackwell, Oklahoma
Israel evacuates 14,000 Ethiopian Jews
New research show impact of 'climate racism' in the US, with black people facing double (+3.12C) the amount of heat stress in urban areas
Barbados elects its first female Prime Minister Mia Mottley, who leads the Barbados Labour Party to victory
The Ostankino Tower in Moscow catches fire for the second time
John Lennontakes delivery of his psychedelically painted Rolls Royce
Gateway Arch in St Louis dedicated
Hands Across America - 6.5 million people hold hands from California to NY
Israel begins to return Sinai to Egypt
Revolution in Mexico overthrows long-time PresidentPorfirio Díaz
The Patriots of Lower Canada (Quebec) rebel against the British for freedom.
Rolling Stones release song "Jumping Jack Flash"
"Beatles Live! At Star-Club in Hamburg Germany" released
Wand Records releases The Isley Brothers' cover single "Twist & Shout"; it becomes their first top-20 hit, peaking at #17
Unicorn by The Irish Rovers hits #7
Jessi Colter [Miriam Johnson], American country singer ("I'm Not Lisa"), born in Phoenix, Arizona
Marshall Allen, American avant garde jazz saxophonist and band leader (Sun Ra Arkestra), born in Louisville, Kentucky[1]
Pieter Zeeman, Dutch physicist (Zeeman effect, Nobel Prize 1902), born in Zonnemaire, Schouwen-Duiveland (d. 1943)
Simon Paap, Dutch dwarf (71 cm/28 inches tall) cabaret artist, born in Zandvoort, Holland (d. 1828)
Princess Helena of the United Kingdom, daughter of Queen Victoria, born in Buckingham Palace, London (d. 1923)
Hal David, American lyricist ("Raindrops Keep Fallin' On My Head"; "What The World Needs Now Is Love"; "Do You Know the Way to San Jose"), born in Brooklyn, New York (d. 2012)
Jack Steinberger, American physicist (1988 Nobel Prize for Physics - Neutrinos), born in Bad Kissingen, Germany (d. 2020)[1]
Beverly Sills [Belle Miriam Silverman], American operatic soprano, born in Brooklyn, New York (d. 2007)
(Joan) "Biddy" Baxter, British television producer and editor best known for her work on "Blue Peter" (1965-1988), born in  Leicester, England (d. 2025)
Tom T. Hall, American country singer, songwriter ("Harper Valley PTA"), and author, born in Olive Hill, Kentucky (d. 2021)
Jamaica Kincaid [Elaine Cynthia Potter Richardsonn], Antiguan-American journalist (NYer), born in St. John's, Antigua and Barbuda
